title: Burnout
lead: Burnout is what chronic, unrelenting stress does when there's never enough recovery. It's not laziness or weakness — it's depletion, and it's reversible.
explanation: Burnout is a state of emotional, physical, and mental exhaustion caused by prolonged stress — most often from work or caregiving. The World Health Organization describes it through three signs: exhaustion, cynicism or detachment, and a drop in your sense of effectiveness.

It builds slowly. You push through, ignore the warning signs, and rely on willpower until the tank is empty. Then things that were once manageable feel impossible, and you may feel numb, resentful, or like you're just going through the motions.
Burnout is a signal, not a failing. Recovering usually needs more than a good night's sleep — it means genuinely reducing the load and rebuilding rest, boundaries, and meaning over time.
symptoms: Dreading tasks you used to manage
Feeling ineffective or that nothing matters
Physical symptoms like headaches or getting sick often

immediate.title: Subtract, don't add
immediate.description: Burnout isn't fixed by doing more self-care on top of an overloaded life. Ask instead: what is one thing I can put down, delegate, or say no to this week?

immediate.title: Rest that actually restores
steps: Notice which kind of rest you're missing — physical, mental, emotional, or social
Choose one small restorative act that matches it
Protect it as non-negotiable, even for 15 minutes

immediate.title: Permission to be a beginner again
immediate.description: Lower the bar on purpose. 'Good enough' is a recovery strategy when you're depleted, not a moral failing.

longTerm.title: Change the conditions
longTerm.description: Because burnout comes from chronic overload, lasting recovery usually requires changing something real — workload, boundaries, role, or support — not just coping harder.

longTerm.title: Reconnect with meaning
longTerm.description: Cynicism eases when you reconnect to why the work or relationships matter, and rebuild small wins that restore your sense of effectiveness.

longTerm.title: Get support early
longTerm.description: Talking to a doctor, therapist, or trusted person helps you see the situation clearly and untangle burnout from depression, which can look similar.

resources.title: Burn-out an 'occupational phenomenon'
resources.whyHere: The definitive description of burnout and its three dimensions.

resources.title: Burnout prevention and treatment
resources.whyHere: Practical, free guide to recognising and recovering from burnout.

resources.title: Burnout: The Secret to Unlocking the Stress Cycle
resources.whyHere: Widely recommended, science-based framework for completing the stress cycle. Check your library for free access.

resources.title: Healthy Minds Program
resources.whyHere: Free training to rebuild resilience and reconnect with purpose.

matchPhrases: i have nothing left to give
i don't care about anything anymore
i can't keep going like this
